title: Add localized HIG Dynamic Type and accessibility checks
summary: Added LocalizationAccessibilityRegression XCUITest class alongside LocalizationLayoutRegression. 4 representative checks: Today shell settles under UICTContentSizeCategoryAccessibilityXL for en + de; root tab buttons expose non-empty accessibility labels under en; root tab buttons remain at ≥44pt hittable width and height under en (Apple HIG minimum). Wired both classes into Makefile ui-regression DOMAIN=localization. No translations touched, no product UI rewritten.
not_claimed: translation quality at any text size
full HIG layout correctness for the 17 LLM-drafted locales
device or TestFlight Dynamic Type behavior
every accessibility label is idiomatic in every locale (only presence and non-emptiness are asserted)
touch target compliance for non-tab-bar controls
residual_risk: Dynamic Type stability is checked for en and de only. Long-script and CJK locales rely on LocalizationLayoutRegression for shell settle but not yet for accessibility XL text-size stability.
Accessibility-label presence is asserted only for the 5 root tab bar buttons. Other surfaces (Today rows, Continue items, Train sessions, Write notes) are not yet covered.
Touch target inspection uses XCUIElement.frame; this matches what Apple HIG documents but does not account for hidden hit regions that SwiftUI may expand beyond the visible frame.
These regressions run in iOS Simulator only. Physical device Dynamic Type and accessibility behavior is not proven by this slice.
